title: Pumpkin pie
subtitle: Spiced pumpkin custard in a shortcrust case
excerpt: A spiced custard of tinned pumpkin purée, cream, eggs, and sugar baked in a blind-baked shortcrust case. The filling sets like a crème caramel: firm at the edges, with a slight wobble at the centre when done.
sourceNotes: Pumpkin pie is documented in Amelia Simmons, American Cookery (1796), the first American cookbook, which enters the public domain. Simmons includes two pumpkin pudding recipes baked in a crust. The spiced custard filling (pumpkin, eggs, cream, sugar, and warm spices) is consistent across American cookery from the early 19th century. The spice mix (cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, allspice) is the standard documented in period sources.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The baked pie keeps refrigerated for 2 days. Serve at room temperature or lightly chilled.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven for the filling bake, after a 200°C blind bake for the case. Fan at 160°C for the filling.

glossaryTerms.definition: A pumpkin pie filling behaves like a baked custard: it is done when the edges are fully set and firm, and the centre still moves slightly as a single unit when the dish is gently shaken. If the entire surface is firm in the oven, the filling will crack as it cools. Remove it at the first sign of a set edge and a gently mobile centre.

content.text: Pumpkin pie is a spiced baked custard. The filling is simple to make and hard to get wrong, but the bake time is critical. The

is the same as for any custard tart: pull it from the oven with some wobble still in the centre. Use tinned pumpkin purée for consistency; fresh roasted pumpkin works but can introduce extra moisture.
Make the pastry and blind bake

Rub the cold butter into the flour and salt until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Add 2 to 3 tablespoons of cold water and mix to a firm dough. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 200°C conventional. Roll the pastry to 3 mm thick and use it to line a 23 cm pie dish, leaving a 1 cm overhang. Line with baking paper and fill with baking beans. Bake for 15 minutes, remove the paper and beans, brush the base with beaten egg yolk, and bake for a further 5 minutes until the base is dry and pale gold. Reduce the oven to 180°C.

Whisk together the pumpkin purée, cream, sugar, eggs, egg yolk, spices, vanilla, and salt until completely smooth. Pour into the blind-baked case. Bake at 180°C for 40 to 45 minutes until the edges are fully set and the centre wobbles gently as a unit when the dish is moved. Leave to cool to room temperature — the filling firms further as it cools. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before slicing. Serve with lightly whipped cream.
